claim: Start with the simplest agent pattern (iterative human-in-the-loop) and scale only when needed; complexity should be earned, not assumed
actionable: Begin with ad-hoc prompts; move to reusable prompts after 3 repetitions; use sub-agents only for parallelization/specialization; upgrade to MCP servers for multi-service integration.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: Reusable prompts (custom commands) are the 80/20 of agent patterns; implement after seeing a workflow repeated 3 times—avoid premature optimization
actionable: After running the same agent workflow 3 times, codify it as a reusable prompt/custom command; version control and iterate on it.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: Sub-agents (Claude Code only) enable parallelization and specialization; use only when you need both; otherwise, reusable prompts suffice
actionable: Use sub-agents to parallelize work (e.g., 5 image generation agents each creating 10 images) or specialize tasks (separate agents for generation vs. editing).
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: Prompt-to-sub-agent orchestration enables workflow reusability; a single prompt spawns multiple specialized sub-agents to solve complex problems in parallel
actionable: Build orchestrator prompts that define workflows; have them spawn sub-agents for different specialized tasks; use results for end-to-end work.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: MCP servers provide the interface layer for agents; wrap external services (APIs, CLIs, internal tools) in MCP servers to expose clean, reusable tools/prompts
actionable: Build MCP server wrappers when integrating multiple external services or exposing proprietary functionality; expose tools and custom prompts for agents to use.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: MCP prompts (custom commands within servers) are underrated; expose common workflows as MCP prompts, not just tools; this multiplies agent effectiveness
actionable: When building MCP servers, define custom prompts alongside tools (e.g., 'generate-and-improve' vs. just 'generate'); let agents discover high-level workflows.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)

claim: Bash dominates actual agent workflows internally, not MCP tool calls; observe what agents actually do before enforcing guardrails
actionable: In production, monitor what agents actually use (bash, MCP, code execution); design constraints based on real patterns, not assumptions.
source_title: 5 Agent PATTERNS to SIMPLIFY Your Agentic Coding (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, MCP)
